evidence_slots.subject: JTAG/debug unlock path and AXI software writer
evidence_slots.operation: clear/override lock state and write lock-control registers
evidence_slots.object: reglk_mem/reglk_ctrl_o lock state consumed by protected wrappers
evidence_slots.expected_guard: jtag_unlock should only be asserted after authenticated JTAG/hash authorization and should not allow unauthenticated clearing/overriding of sticky lock bits; software writes should be gated by access-control and existing lock bits
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: reglk_wrapper assigns reglk_ctrl_o from reglk_mem. Its sequential block resets every reglk_mem entry to zero when ~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9), meaning assertion of jtag_unlock clears the lock registers. Normal writes are gated by en=en_acct&&acct_ctrl_i and selected reglk_ctrl_i bits, but jtag_unlock bypasses those per-register write guards through the reset/clear condition.
evidence_slots.path: riscv_peripherals dmi_jtag.jtag_unlock_o -> top-level jtag_unlock -> i_reglk_wrapper.jtag_unlock -> reglk_mem clear -> reglk_ctrl_o -> protected wrapper reglk_ctrl_i slices
evidence_slots.state_condition: rst_ni asserted, rst_9 deasserted, lock bits set in reglk_mem, jtag_unlock asserted
evidence_slots.impact: Clearing lock bits can re-enable writes/readback to crypto, reset, accounting, packet/fuse-related and other protected registers that rely on reglk_ctrl_i, potentially bypassing intended sticky write-protection after software has locked the design.

reglk_wrapper instance connects .jtag_unlock(jtag_unlock), reglk_ctrl_o(reglk_ctrl), acct_ctrl_i(acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][9]), and rst_9
reglk_ctrl_o is composed from reglk_mem entries; reglk_ctrl_i is used as local lock control
if ~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9), all reglk_mem entries are assigned 'h0
normal AXI writes are gated by en&&we and lock bits, unlike the jtag_unlock clear path

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: The internals of dmi_jtag/hash challenge were not provided, so the strength of jtag_unlock authentication is uncertain. However, the lock-clear semantics on jtag_unlock are directly observed.
inspection_records.candidate_finding: reglk_wrapper clears all exported lock state whenever jtag_unlock is asserted, so the debug unlock path can reset/clear lock bits rather than merely enabling an authenticated maintenance path; provided evidence does not show that jtag_unlock is only asserted after an authorization protocol strong enough to justify clearing sticky locks.
inspection_records.notes: This is a candidate permission vulnerability because the debug unlock signal is wired as a lock-state clearing condition.

evidence_slots.subject: AXI/NoC requester to AES wrappers
evidence_slots.object: AES key registers, plaintext/state/control registers, and ciphertext/result outputs
evidence_slots.expected_guard: AES key/control/plaintext writes and reads of key/internal state should be mediated by acct_ctrl_i, reglk_ctrl_i, and debug_mode_i restrictions where applicable
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: AES wrappers gate local enable with en_acct && acct_ctrl_i and use reglk_ctrl_i bits on many writes/reads. However, AES0 read logic initializes rdata to key0[address[8:3]] whenever en is true before handling explicit cases, and default only forces zero if ct_valid. This creates a readback path for key0 entries at key-address indices not otherwise overwritten by the case. AES1 also has explicit key register readback controlled by key_we* address-decoder signals and reglk_ctrl_i[2]. Debug mode zeroizes some core key inputs, but does not itself remove all MMIO key readback paths.
evidence_slots.path: NoC AXI-lite bridge -> aes0/aes1/aes2 wrapper axi_req_i -> axi_lite_interface -> en/we/address -> key/control/state registers -> rdata mux and AES core inputs
evidence_slots.state_condition: acct_ctrl_i enabled, reglk read-lock bits unset, debug_mode_i asserted/deasserted, AES wrapper enabled by AXI access
evidence_slots.impact: An authorized-at-accounting-level but otherwise unprivileged or post-lock requester may read AES key material if lock bits are not correctly set or are cleared, compromising cryptographic confidentiality and enabling decryption/forgery of protected data.
